title: Challenge: fix the broken Alloy scrape

blocks.content:
# Fix the broken Prometheus scrape

This is a **challenge**, not a guided tutorial. You'll be dropped into a virtual machine where Alloy is supposed to be scraping a local Node Exporter and forwarding metrics to a Prometheus running on the same VM. **It isn't.**

Your job is to diagnose the misconfiguration and fix it. Click _Check my work_ when you think metrics are flowing again. Hints are available, but try to figure it out yourself first.

blocks.title: Restore the metrics pipeline
blocks.brief: Alloy is running on this VM and should be scraping `localhost:9100` (Node Exporter) and remote-writing to the local Prometheus at `localhost:9090`. Right now, Prometheus has no series for the `up` metric. Fix the Alloy configuration so metrics start flowing again.
blocks.setupScript:
sudo sed -i 's|http://localhost:9100/metrics|http://localhost:9101/metrics|' /etc/alloy/config.alloy
sudo systemctl restart alloy

hintLevels.text: Start by checking whether Alloy is actually running. `systemctl status alloy` is a good first stop.
Alloy is running but its config might be wrong. Open `/etc/alloy/config.alloy` and look at the scrape target's URL.
Node Exporter listens on port `9100`. The Alloy config currently points somewhere else. Fix the URL, then `sudo systemctl restart alloy`.

blocks.failureMessage: Prometheus still has no live `up` series. Either Alloy isn't scraping, isn't running, or its remote_write isn't reaching Prometheus.

blocks.content:
## Nice work

If you solved that, you've practiced the core debugging loop for an Alloy + Prometheus pipeline: check the agent, check its config, check the target, verify the result in the metrics backend. Same shape applies to most observability incidents.
